page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on the Neoverse-N1 architecture, with 64.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor and includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage. Network performance is anchored by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks place the c6gd.8xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. It achieves a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1894.03 GB/sec for cached small block reads and 173.34 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0 and a gzip compression rate of 20.31 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers competitive resource density, offering 32 dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading alongside high-speed local NVMe storage. This makes the instance highly suitable for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space and parallel CPU processing. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Operating with a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the instance delivers balanced multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0. While single-core performance is comparatively modest, its high-speed local storage and dedicated core allocation offer excellent resource density for parallel tasks. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance parallel workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for parallel workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core performance with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
